predicted_value: Contractor shall comply with all of the requirements applicable to Contractor, or assigned to Contractor by Owner, as provided in the Building Information Modeling Requirements (“BIM Requirements”) attached hereto as Exhibit L. Owner and Contractor acknowledge and agree that neither the Building Information Model nor any Model Element (as said terms are defined in the BIM Requirements) is a Contract Document and does not alter or supersede in any respect the Drawings and Specifications or any other design document prepared by Architect and its consultants. Without limitation of the foregoing, Contractor shall throughout the performance of the Work maintain accurate and current electronic “as built” records of all material changes in the Work made by Contractor or its Subcontractors in the field from what is shown on the Construction Documents, as the same may be modified during the course of the Work in accordance with the terms of the Contract Documents, and 3D shop drawings, construction coordination models and all other documentation required by Exhibit L hereto so that Architect may employ such records to prepare record drawings in BIM format. As a condition precedent to Owner’s obligation to make payment with respect to any Application for Payment, Contractor shall demonstrate to Owner and Architect that Contractor’s records as aforesaid are accurate and correct, and upon request from time to time by Owner or Architect, shall deliver to Architect complete copies of such records, active files of which shall be maintained at the Project site. Nothing contained herein shall authorize any changes in the Work except as authorized by a duly executed Change Order.

Insurance proceeds received by the Contractor also accrue to the Owner. These amounts are credited to the Owner as a deduction from the Cost of the Work.

question: What is the Cost of the Work Article #?
What is the definition for the Cost of the Work?
gold_value: Task Order Exhibit I - wages of workers, contractor's supervisory and admin personnel per exhibit J, taxes, insurance, subcontractor cost, transportation and storage cost, cost of materials, rental equipment, temp facilities, debris removal, contractor expenses, permit fees, testing fees, deposits lost, cost incurred due to emergencies
predicted_value: The term 'Cost of the Work' shall mean costs necessarily incurred by the Contractor, directly or in connection with Subcontracts, in good faith and in the proper performance of the Work. The Cost of the Work shall include only the following items: wages of construction workers, wages or salaries of supervisory and administrative personnel, costs for taxes, insurance, contributions, assessments and benefits, payments to Subcontractors, costs of materials and equipment, rental charges for temporary facilities, machinery, equipment, and hand tools, costs of removal of debris, costs of document reproductions and postage, travel expenses, insurance and bond premiums, sales and use taxes, fees and assessments for permits, licenses and inspections, fees of testing laboratories, deposits lost for causes other than the Contractor's negligence, other costs approved in advance in writing by the Owner, and costs due to emergencies.

question: What are the Final Payment Terms & Conditions?
gold_value: arch to certify. final sub waivers. insurance verification during period required in contract documents
predicted_value: The final payment terms and conditions are as follows: The Contract Sum is the lesser of the Cost of the Work plus the Contractor’s Fee or the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor’s Fee is 3% of the Cost of the Work, subject to the Guaranteed Maximum Price limitation. The Guaranteed Maximum Price is $2,822,335. Adjustments to the Contract Sum on account of changes in the Work shall be determined in accordance with Article 7 of the Master Terms and Conditions. All payments made by the Owner pursuant to the Task Order shall be credited against the Guaranteed Maximum Price. The Contractor shall maintain accurate and current electronic 'as built' records of all material changes in the Work. As a condition precedent to Owner’s obligation to make payment with respect to any Application for Payment, Contractor shall demonstrate to Owner and Architect that Contractor’s records are accurate and correct. The Cost of the Work shall not include any bonds obtained for Subcontractors covered by Contractor’s Subcontractor Default Insurance policy. Contractor shall comply with all BIM Requirements. The Contractor shall not remove machinery or equipment from the Project site without the Owner’s prior written approval. Costs of complying with the BIM Requirements, removal of debris, document reproductions, postage, parcel delivery charges, travel expenses, insurance, bond premiums, sales and use taxes, fees for permits, licenses, inspections, testing laboratories, and other costs incurred in the performance of the Work if approved in advance in writing by the Owner are included. Costs due to emergencies incurred in taking action to prevent threatened damage, injury, or loss are also included. The Contractor shall not duplicate payments for any items comprising the Cost of the Work. The Cost of the Work shall not include salaries and other compensation of the Contractor's personnel, expenses of the Contractor's principal office, overhead and general expenses, capital expenses, rental costs of machinery and equipment, and costs due to the fault or negligence of the Contractor or Subcontractors.
explanation: The predicted answer provides detailed payment terms and conditions, but it does not cover the specific elements mentioned in the gold answer such as final sub waivers, insurance verification, and arch to certify.
